diagrams.title: Spiral Model Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the four quadrants of the spiral: Planning, Risk Analysis, Engineering, and Evaluation arranged in a spiral pattern expanding outward through iterations
elements: Risk analysis points marked on each loop
Angular sections showing the four phases
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how each iteration increases completeness while managing risks

diagrams.title: Comparison: Waterfall vs Evolutionary
diagrams.description: Side-by-side representation of sequential waterfall (straight path) vs evolutionary (cyclical/iterative path)
elements: Waterfall: Requirements → Design → Implementation → Testing → Deployment → Maintenance (linear)
Evolutionary: Initial concept → Prototype → Feedback → Refined prototype → More features → Final system (cyclical)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the fundamental difference in approach between sequential and evolutionary models

diagrams.title: Prototyping Process Flow
diagrams.description: Flow diagram showing the prototyping lifecycle from requirement gathering to final system
diagrams.purpose: Understand how prototypes are built, evaluated, and refined iteratively

visuals.title: Evolutionary Process Models
visuals.description: Visual diagram for evolutionary process models
